texto: I don't think there is anything like the awareness of space to process emotion. That space is such an incredible processor. There is no analysis equal to the processing capacity of open awareness. When you are trying to analyze something, you don't realize that the analyzer itself is part of the problem. Both the problem and the analyzer are constructions of the mind. But direct, open, naked awareness is not a construction of the mind but the nature of the mind itself, and therefore the greatest processor ever.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tenzin Wangyal Rinpoche: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't think there is anything like the awareness of space to process emotion. That space is suc…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, awareness, emotions pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de emotions e mind.

There is no analysis equal to the processing capacity of open awareness. When you are trying to analyze something, you don't realize that the analyzer itself is part of the problem. Both the problem and the analyzer are constructions of the mind. But direct, open, naked awareness is not a construction of the mind but the nature of the mind itself, and therefore the greatest processor ever.
